manifest.name: Alert
manifest.description: The Alert component is a versatile UI element designed to display prominent, context-sensitive messages to users. It's ideal for conveying critical information such as warnings, errors, success notifications, or general informational updates that require immediate user attention without being blocking. Key features include support for different visual variants (default, destructive, success, warning) to semantically indicate the message type, and optional icon integration to enhance clarity. Use cases include form validation feedback, system status updates, confirmation messages after user actions, or drawing attention to important announcements within an application interface.

valueDescriptions.default: Standard informational alert with a neutral appearance.
valueDescriptions.destructive: Alert indicating a critical error or a dangerous action, typically red in color.
valueDescriptions.success: Alert indicating a successful operation or positive feedback, typically green in color.
valueDescriptions.warning: Alert indicating a potential issue or caution, typically yellow/orange in color.

minimal.title: Default
minimal.code:
(
    <Alert className="nx:max-w-md">
      <AlertTitle>Heads up!</AlertTitle>
      <AlertDescription>
        You can add components and dependencies to your app using the CLI.
      </AlertDescription>
    </Alert>
  )

common.title: Destructive
common.code:
(
    <Alert variant="destructive" className="nx:max-w-md">
      <AlertTitle>Error</AlertTitle>
      <AlertDescription>
        Your session has expired. Please log in again.
      </AlertDescription>
    </Alert>
  )

common.title: Success
common.code:
(
    <Alert variant="success" className="nx:max-w-md">
      <AlertTitle>Success</AlertTitle>
      <AlertDescription>
        Your changes have been saved successfully.
      </AlertDescription>
    </Alert>
  )

common.title: Warning
common.code:
(
    <Alert variant="warning" className="nx:max-w-md">
      <AlertTitle>Warning</AlertTitle>
      <AlertDescription>
        Your account is about to expire. Please renew your subscription.
      </AlertDescription>
    </Alert>
  )

common.title: With Icon
common.code:
(
    <Alert className="nx:max-w-md">
      <IconInfoCircle className="nx:size-4" />
      <AlertTitle>Information</AlertTitle>
      <AlertDescription>
        This is an informational alert with an icon.
      </AlertDescription>
    </Alert>
  )

common.title: Destructive With Icon
common.code:
(
    <Alert variant="destructive" className="nx:max-w-md">
      <IconAlertCircle className="nx:size-4" />
      <AlertTitle>Error</AlertTitle>
      <AlertDescription>
        Something went wrong. Please try again later.
      </AlertDescription>
    </Alert>
  )

common.title: Success With Icon
common.code:
(
    <Alert variant="success" className="nx:max-w-md">
      <IconCircleCheck className="nx:size-4" />
      <AlertTitle>Success</AlertTitle>
      <AlertDescription>
        Your payment was processed successfully.
      </AlertDescription>
    </Alert>
  )

common.title: Warning With Icon
common.code:
(
    <Alert variant="warning" className="nx:max-w-md">
      <IconAlertTriangle className="nx:size-4" />
      <AlertTitle>Warning</AlertTitle>
      <AlertDescription>
        Your storage is almost full. Consider upgrading your plan.
      </AlertDescription>
    </Alert>
  )

guidance.whenToUse: Use to display important, contextual messages to users, such as warnings, errors, success confirmations, or general informational notices that require immediate attention. It provides a non-intrusive way to communicate status or feedback.
guidance.whenNotToUse: Avoid using for purely decorative purposes or for persistent information that doesn't require user attention; use plain text or other display components instead. Do not use for critical, blocking actions where a modal or dialog would be more appropriate.
guidance.accessibility: Ensure appropriate ARIA roles (e.g., 'alert' for urgent, time-sensitive information, or 'status' for less urgent advice) are applied based on the urgency and nature of the message. Provide clear, concise text content. If an icon is used, ensure it is either purely decorative (and hidden from screen readers) or provides meaningful context via accessible labels.
